definition: Fundamental not-knowing that obscures the nature of experience and conditions the arising of suffering.
notes: The project prefers ignorance as the default because it is readable and established, while the notes and context rules keep it from sounding like mere lack of information. Not-knowing can be useful when the active, ongoing failure to see clearly needs emphasis.

context_rules.context: In most doctrinal and dependent-origination contexts
In explanatory prose emphasizing the literal force of the term
context_rules.notes: Useful when clarifying that the term is not only intellectual ignorance.

context_rules.context: In prose stressing obscured awareness rather than simple absence of information
context_rules.notes: Acceptable alternate when it reads more naturally.

example_phrases.notes: Opening link in the standard dependent-arising sequence.

example_phrases.translation: but with the complete fading and quenching of ignorance
example_phrases.notes: Shows the term in the cessation mode of the formula.
